content:
- Verified the explicit ripgrep acceptance gate, `cargo build --workspace`, `cargo test --workspace`, `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after the setup-sql deletion and harness/doc cleanup.
- Marked the story-28 task completed with passes=true and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which updated the Ralph ledgers and sent the task-finished notification email.
- Next I am staging the full worktree, committing with the required task-finished message including the green-lane evidence, and pushing the branch.

- Read the previous progress log, refreshed Ralph task summaries, read the chooser rules, and inspected the three active task files plus the workspace layout.
- Confirmed there are no active unblocked bug tasks and no explicit <blocked_by> gates on the three active failing tasks; story 28 task 01 is already complete, task 03 depends on the docs contract from task 02, and the repo currently has no `scripts/` directory or `docs/setup_sql/` docs yet.
- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` with `.ralph/tasks/story-28-replace-setup-sql-with-docs-and-bash-scripts/02-task-write-setup-sql-documentation.md` as the most logical next unblocked failing task; next agent should execute that docs task, then continue story 28 with task 03 before switching stories unless a higher-priority bug appears.

- Read prior codex progress, confirmed the current task pointer is story 28 task 02 setup-sql documentation, and loaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed this turn must follow the task file state next: either create/update a plan and quit, or execute an existing approved plan.
- Next I am reading the task file and the surrounding story files to determine the exact required deliverable and whether execution is allowed yet.

- Read the story 28 task 02 file. It is still `not_started` with no `NOW EXECUTE` marker, so this turn must create a plan file in the task-specific plans directory and quit immediately afterward.
- The task is docs-only, so the TDD exception matters: I should plan verification through executable or lintable behavior where possible rather than brittle text-string tests.
- Next I am extracting the actual SQL contract and variable names from the repo so the plan can name the exact docs structure, examples, and validation approach.

- Read the story-28 task-02 requirements, inspected the live SQL/grant contract in `crates/ingest-contract`, `crates/runner/tests/support/e2e_harness.rs`, `crates/runner/tests/support/multi_mapping_harness.rs`, `crates/runner/src/postgres_bootstrap.rs`, and the bootstrap/reconcile tests.
- Created the execution-ready plan `.ralph/tasks/story-28-replace-setup-sql-with-docs-and-bash-scripts/02-task-write-setup-sql-documentation_plans/2026-04-27-setup-sql-docs-plan.md` and linked it from the task file with a `<plan>` tag.
- The plan leaves the task in `NOW EXECUTE` state and locks the docs contract to the current public behavior: `MappingIngestPath`, changefeed SQL options, one-time cursor capture, and PostgreSQL grants/helper-schema bootstrap.
- Next agent should read the task file and linked plan, execute the docs creation in `docs/setup_sql/`, then run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, update the task checkboxes and passes flag, switch task, commit, and push if all lanes pass.

- Read the previous codex progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill files.
- Confirmed the active task is story 28 task 02 and previous progress indicates an approved execution plan already exists for this turn.
- Next I am reading the task file, linked plan, and boundary-skill directory contents to verify whether I should execute immediately and what docs/test contract must be preserved.

- Read the task file and linked plan; the plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn should fully implement the docs and finish the task if all required lanes pass.
- Verified the plan targets only `docs/setup_sql/` plus task metadata, and the boundary-cleanup goal is to turn scattered SQL literals into one operator-facing canonical docs boundary.
- Next I am extracting the exact source-side changefeed SQL, ingest-path formatting, destination grant contract, and a couple of boundary-skill smell notes before writing the new docs.

- Confirmed execution mode and extracted the main public contracts: ingest paths come from `MappingIngestPath`, source SQL comes from the e2e harnesses, and destination grants/helper-schema behavior comes from `postgres_bootstrap.rs` plus bootstrap/reconcile tests.
- The boundary-skill guidance points toward fixing the current wrong-place boundary by moving this setup knowledge into one explicit docs boundary instead of leaving it scattered across tests and README fragments.
- Next I am reading the exact SQL snippets and the task-03 YAML/SQL sample so the new templates use the same variable names and statement ordering the repo already expects.

- Confirmed the exact CockroachDB SQL contract: enable rangefeeds, capture one cursor per source database, and create changefeeds pointing at `webhook-<base>/ingest/<mapping_id>?ca_cert=<encoded>` with `initial_scan='yes'`, `envelope='enriched'`, `enriched_properties='source'`, and `resolved='<interval>'`.
- Confirmed the exact PostgreSQL privilege contract: `GRANT CONNECT, CREATE ON DATABASE`, `GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA`, and `GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON TABLE`, while the runtime itself creates `_cockroach_migration_tool` and tracking tables.
- Next I am creating `docs/setup_sql/` and writing the three markdown guides, then I will manually compare them back to the source contracts before running the required lanes.

- Created `docs/setup_sql/index.md`, `docs/setup_sql/cockroachdb-source-setup.md`, and `docs/setup_sql/postgresql-destination-grants.md` as the new canonical operator-facing SQL contract.
- The source guide now documents the exact changefeed options, `/ingest/<mapping_id>` path, URL-safe `ca_cert` encoding requirement, cursor-capture workflow, and a two-database worked example; the destination guide documents the exact grant contract and runtime-owned helper schema behavior.
- Next I am reviewing the rendered markdown against the live Rust/test contracts and then I will run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` before marking the task complete.

- Wrote the three `docs/setup_sql/` guides and completed a manual contract review against `MappingIngestPath`, the e2e changefeed SQL, and the PostgreSQL bootstrap/grant tests.
- `make check` passed and `make lint` passed. `make test` is currently running through the integration contract suite.
- Next I will wait for `make test` to finish, then update the task checklist and completion metadata, switch task, commit, and push if the lane stays green.
